topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, a bright, unwelcome intrusion on my otherwise quiet evening. It was a direct message from Mr. Harrison. My heart gave a sudden, heavy thump against my ribs. I clicked it open, expecting a mundane reminder about an assignment, but the words swam before my eyes. He had tagged me in a photo from the school event—a picture I hadn't even realized he had taken. A wave of intense awkwardness washed over me, making my palms instantly slick. I quickly minimized the window, my fingers trembling slightly as I tried to regain a semblance of composure. The weight of his attention felt enormous, pressing down on my chest.

[story 2]

I stared at my phone screen, the glow illuminating the anxious lines around my eyes. It was impossible. Professor Evans, the sternest man in the department, had somehow found my profile and started liking my posts. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, a feeling of profound inadequacy creeping in. I scrolled through his recent activity, each interaction feeling like a spotlight aimed squarely at my shortcomings. I felt a desperate urge to hide under my duvet, to disappear entirely. My breath hitched, shallow and quick, as I replayed the interaction in my head, trying to find a rational explanation for this bewildering, unwanted connection.

[story 3]

The email arrived with a subject line that made my stomach clench: "Quick question regarding the history project." Inside, it wasn't just a query; it was a direct link to my Instagram story from two days prior. Seeing his familiar face in the background, smiling faintly, sent a jolt of sheer panic through me. I felt my face flush a deep crimson, wishing the floor would swallow me whole. I slowly put the phone down on the desk, my hands shaking so badly I had to grip the edge of the wood. The silence of my room amplified the frantic drumming of my pulse as I wrestled with the unexpected reality of his digital presence.
